briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of Banking-as-a-Service middleware provider Synapse, raising serious questions about consumer protections in embedded finance.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving a new wave of unconventional credit card products that challenge traditional banking norms.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of open banking startup Credit Kudos signals Big Tech's deepening interest in leveraging financial data for consumer products.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A detailed analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Koho close to securing banking licence, hopes to launch telco product, CEO says](https://betakit.com/koho-hopes-to-launch-telco/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian neobank Koho is on the verge of obtaining a full banking licence and is exploring a bold expansion into telecommunications services.

- **[Canada committed to real-time rail, aims to be a point of stability in an uncertain world, finance minister says](https://betakit.com/canada-committed-to-real-time-rail-aims-to-be-a-point-of-stability-in-an-uncertain-world-finance-minister-says/)** — *BetaKit* — Canada's finance minister reaffirmed the country's commitment to building a real-time payments rail, positioning Canada as a reliable partner amid global economic uncertainty.

- **[Moomoo Canada Partners with Nasdaq to Launch Canada's First Live, Real-Money National Trading Competition](https://financialpost.com/globe-newswire/moomoo-canada-partners-with-nasdaq-to-launch-canadas-first-live-real-money-national-trading-competition)** — *Financial Post* — Moomoo Canada and Nasdaq are launching Canada's first live national trading competition with a $1 million prize pool, targeting self-directed retail investors with AI-driven tools.

- **[It's a tough market for liquidity. Here's where companies and investors are turning](https://betakit.com/its-a-tough-market-for-liquidity-heres-where-companies-and-investors-are-turning/)** — *BetaKit* — With traditional exit routes constrained, Canadian companies and investors are exploring alternative liquidity strategies in a challenging venture market.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — *(Evergreen relevance)* Canada's early push to phase out screen scraping in favour of secure open banking APIs remains a foundational piece of understanding the country's ongoing financial data reform journey.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A provocative look at the darker implications of fintech's rapid expansion, raising important questions about consumer protection, financial inclusion, and the risks of unchecked innovation — themes directly relevant to building a responsible fintech ecosystem in Atlantic Canada.

- **[OpenAI violated Canadian privacy laws, federal and provincial watchdogs say](https://betakit.com/federal-and-provincial-privacy-watchdogs-say-openai-violated-canadian-privacy-laws/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian federal and provincial privacy regulators found OpenAI in violation of domestic privacy laws, a significant signal for Atlantic Canadian fintechs navigating AI adoption and data governance compliance.

- **[Bobbie Racette built her new startup around a simple idea: be the support she never had](https://betakit.com/bobbie-racette-built-her-new-startup-around-a-simple-idea-be-the-support-she-never-had/)** — *BetaKit* — Indigenous entrepreneur Bobbie Racette's new venture highlights the growing importance of inclusive, founder-first ecosystem building — an inspiring model for Atlantic Canada's own emerging startup community.
